annotation_items.text: the way you use not this way
annotation_items.correction: you don't use it this way
annotation_items.explanation: Negation and word order are incorrect; place 'not' after auxiliary ('don't') and include the object 'it'.

annotation_items.text: I try to say it again
annotation_items.correction: I'll try to say it again
annotation_items.explanation: Use a future/modal form ('I'll try' or 'let me') to express an intention to repeat your explanation.
The noun phrase needs a definite article: 'the MVP version'.

annotation_items.text: now user flow a bit complicated to work with it week to week
annotation_items.correction: the user flow is a bit complicated to work with on a week-to-week basis
annotation_items.explanation: Missing article and verb ('the' and 'is'), and 'with it week to week' is awkward — use 'work with on a week-to-week basis'.

annotation_items.text: when I need to put script
annotation_items.correction: when I needed to put a script
annotation_items.explanation: After 'before' the action is in the past, and 'script' needs an article.
The verb 'keep' needs a direct object ('it') to complete the idea.

annotation_items.text: currently I Resolve the main pain
annotation_items.correction: currently I have resolved the main pain
annotation_items.explanation: With 'currently' the present perfect is more appropriate to show a recently completed result.

annotation_items.text: lack of database of our sessions
annotation_items.correction: the lack of a database for our sessions
annotation_items.explanation: Missing articles ('the', 'a') and 'for' is more natural than 'of' with 'database' here.

annotation_items.text: It's easy and interesting metric grammar error rate
annotation_items.correction: It's an easy and interesting metric: the grammar error rate
annotation_items.explanation: Missing article 'an' before 'easy' and the phrase needs a clearer link to 'grammar error rate'.

annotation_items.text: It's understandable and very useful metric as well
annotation_items.correction: It's an understandable and very useful metric as well
annotation_items.explanation: Missing indefinite article 'an' before 'understandable'.

annotation_items.text: it's the two metric that would be would be enough
annotation_items.correction: these two metrics would be enough
annotation_items.explanation: 'Metric' should be plural ('metrics') and a more natural phrasing is 'these two metrics'.

annotation_items.text: not metric but approach Maybe top three
annotation_items.correction: not a metric but an approach — maybe top three
annotation_items.explanation: Missing articles ('a', 'an') before 'metric' and 'approach'; punctuation/capitalization also needed.

annotation_items.text: your most usual for you or for me mistakes
annotation_items.correction: your or my most common mistakes
annotation_items.explanation: Word order and choice are awkward; use 'your or my most common mistakes' for clarity.

annotation_items.text: I tried to make to realize that
annotation_items.explanation: Redundant verb 'make' plus 'to' creates an ungrammatical sequence; use 'tried to realize'.
Adverb 'correctly' is required after the verb 'works'.

annotation_items.text: Present here step by step analyze of every line
annotation_items.correction: present here a step-by-step analysis of every line
annotation_items.explanation: Use the noun 'analysis' (not the verb 'analyze') and include the article 'a'.

annotation_items.correction: Currently I reduce some of the pain
annotation_items.explanation: 'Decrease' with 'pain' is an unusual collocation; 'reduce some of the pain' is more natural.
Count noun 'example' should be plural after 'a lot of'.
Missing definite article 'the' before 'same mistakes'.
Count noun 'mistake' requires an article here ('a').
The speaker means the grammatical term 'auxiliary verbs' rather than 'simple verbs'.
Missing indefinite article 'an' before a singular count noun 'objective metric'.
Tense mismatch: 'currently' implies present simple ('exclude'), not past ('excluded').
Comparative 'slower' is incorrect here; you want 'lower' (a lower rate), not 'slower'.
In English the structure is 'too low a [noun]'; the article 'a' is required.
Noun should be plural to agree with the plural predicate (referring to multiple sessions).
Subject-verb agreement: 'it is' cannot be used with the plural noun 'metrics'.
Missing article before the singular countable noun 'progress bar'.
The verb 'sent' here requires a direct object (what was sent).

annotation_items.text: just progress bar will be presented here
annotation_items.correction: just the progress bar will be presented here
annotation_items.explanation: Missing article before singular noun 'progress bar' (use 'the' or 'a').
Missing article before the noun; the phrase is a fragment and needs 'a' to be grammatical.
In time clauses ('when'), English normally uses the present simple rather than 'will'.
Wrong preposition: English uses possessive phrasing ('our mistakes') or 'mistakes we make' rather than 'mistakes from us'.
Plural subject 'missing articles' requires the plural verb 'are'.
Sentence is missing a verb and article; reorder to a full clause ('the structure is unclear').

annotation_items.text: I'm not agree with second one
annotation_items.correction: I don't agree with the second one
annotation_items.explanation: Incorrect verb form: English uses 'don't agree' rather than 'I'm not agree'.
Unnatural collocation: 'rude mistake' is a literal transfer; 'serious' or 'major' is the natural collocation in this context.
Wrong preposition: collocation is 'work on' something rather than 'work with' in this context.
Use the preposition 'on' with 'depend' (depend on something), not 'by'.

annotation_items.text: I think you just can try
annotation_items.explanation: Adverb placement: in this structure 'just' should come after the modal ('can') or be omitted — 'you can just try' or 'you can try'.

annotation_items.text: to illustrate it could sound like
annotation_items.correction: to illustrate what it could sound like
annotation_items.explanation: The clause needs a complementizer ('what') to introduce the indirect clause: 'to illustrate what it could sound like.'
Subject–verb agreement: 'it's' (it is) does not agree with plural noun 'mistakes'; use 'they're' or 'they are'.

annotation_items.text: API can see whole amount of text
annotation_items.correction: API can see the whole amount of text
annotation_items.explanation: Missing article 'the' before 'whole amount'.
Count noun 'summary' requires an article here ('a summary') or use the verb 'summarize'.

annotation_items.text: and try to find some errors
annotation_items.correction: and tries to find some errors
annotation_items.explanation: Subject–verb agreement: the subject is 'it' earlier, so the verb should be 'tries' to agree with singular subject.
Redundant repetition of two near-synonyms; use one noun ('errors' or 'mistakes').
Wrong article combination: 'the some' is ungrammatical — use 'some' (no 'the') or 'the patterns'.
Missing linking verb: include 'is' (contracted to 'that's') to form 'I suppose that's correct.'

annotation_items.correction: I'm convinced that / It is evident that
annotation_items.explanation: 'Evident' doesn't collocate with 'I' in this structure; use 'I'm convinced' or 'It is evident that'.
'Transcribation' is not the correct noun; the correct word is 'transcription'.
Subject ('it' implied) is singular, so the verb should be 'seems' (subject-verb agreement).
Embedded question requires statement word order ('where the point is'), not question order.

annotation_items.text: how it would say this one.
annotation_items.correction: how to say this / how you would say this
annotation_items.explanation: Missing/incorrect subject and word order; use 'how to say this' or 'how you would say this'.

annotation_items.text: in this block should be only rude mistakes
annotation_items.correction: there should only be rude mistakes in this block
annotation_items.explanation: Word order is incorrect for English; put the verb before or use 'there should be...'.
'Root mistakes' is not a standard collocation; use 'root causes' or 'fundamental mistakes'.
Use the adjective 'recurring' to describe repeating mistakes; 'recurrence' is the noun form and is incorrect here.
'Irrepeatable' is not a standard word in this context; use 'irreproducible' or 'not repeatable'.

annotation_items.correction: in an ideal scenario / in an ideal world
annotation_items.explanation: 'Ideal picture' is a calque from other languages; English prefers 'ideal scenario' or 'ideal world'.
Subject-verb agreement and article: plural 'mistakes' requires 'are' (and no 'a').
Missing definite article before 'correct way'.
Article-number mismatch: 'a' cannot be used with plural 'blocks'.
Negative contexts with 'wouldn't' normally use 'either' rather than 'too'.
Missing subject 'you' and incorrect word order for the question. Capitalization of 'Complex' should be lowercase in this context.
In time clauses in English, do not use 'will' — use present simple ('when you take').
Demonstrative must agree in number with the plural noun ('these' + 'errors').
Missing definite article before a specific noun phrase; say 'in the text'.

annotation_items.correction: a description of the mistake and suggestions for improvement
annotation_items.explanation: Missing articles and unnatural noun phrase; use 'a description of the mistake' and a plural/phrase for improvements.

annotation_items.text: I'm just confusing that it will be easy to
annotation_items.correction: I'm just confused that it will be easy to develop
annotation_items.explanation: Wrong verb/participle: 'confusing' (active) should be the adjective 'confused' to express the speaker's state.
Missing definite article before an identifiable system: 'the API'.
Unnatural collocation: use 'a large amount (of)' or 'large amounts' rather than 'big amount'.
Missing definite article before a specific API: 'the API'.

annotation_items.text: Don't have an opportunity to make it still
annotation_items.correction: I still don't have an opportunity to do it
annotation_items.explanation: Missing subject/auxiliary and unnatural word order; add 'I' and use natural phrasing 'still don't have an opportunity to do it'.
Incomplete object after 'process our' — the noun is missing (e.g. 'speech', 'recording', 'conversation').
Incorrect use of 'let's' with a first-person object: use the imperative 'Tell me...' or restructure the sentence.

annotation_items.text: Is she leave her work or not?
annotation_items.correction: Has she left her job or not?
annotation_items.explanation: Wrong tense/form and verb structure for a past action; use present perfect (or simple past) + past participle 'left'.

annotation_items.correction: You said about vacation / You mentioned a vacation
annotation_items.explanation: Wrong word: 'vocation' (occupation) vs intended 'vacation' (time off).

annotation_items.correction: She left because / She left altogether because
annotation_items.explanation: Incorrect use of 'at all' here; likely intended 'altogether' or nothing—'left because' is correct.

annotation_items.text: there is no salary in last month
annotation_items.correction: there was no salary last month
annotation_items.explanation: Wrong tense and preposition: refer to a past month with past tense ('was') and no 'in': 'last month'.

annotation_items.text: didn't pay for the part of salary for the December
annotation_items.correction: didn't pay part of her salary for December
annotation_items.explanation: Wrong word order and articles: say 'pay part of her salary' and use 'for December' (no 'the').

annotation_items.text: take a small gap like two weeks
annotation_items.correction: take a short break for/about two weeks
annotation_items.explanation: Unnatural collocation: 'small gap' is not the usual collocation for time off; use 'short break' or 'a gap of two weeks'.
Unnatural verb choice/collocation: native speakers say 'recharge' or 'rest', not 'reload' for recuperation.
Verb form: after 'start' use the -ing form for continuous action ('start working').
After 'like' you need the object form of the pronoun ('her'), not the subject form 'she'.
'People' is the correct plural; 'peoples' is used only for groups/nations, not counting individuals.
Use 'with' (small talk is done with someone), not 'to'.
Use 'translate ... into ...' to indicate language conversion; 'in' is incorrect here.

annotation_items.text: I just wanted to use to explain the word faster
annotation_items.correction: I just wanted to use it to explain the word faster
annotation_items.explanation: The verb 'use' needs an object here ('it') before the infinitive 'to explain'.

annotation_items.text: I need to know that we finished
annotation_items.correction: I need to know that we've finished
annotation_items.explanation: Use present perfect ('we've finished') to show a completed action relevant to now; simple past sounds less natural here.

annotation_items.text: You told me we have 31 minute already
annotation_items.correction: You told me we have 31 minutes already
annotation_items.explanation: 'minute' needs plural form 'minutes' to match the number '31'.

annotation_items.correction: I suggest (that) you speak a little bit more
annotation_items.explanation: After 'suggest' use the base verb (or 'suggest that + subject + verb'), not 'to' + infinitive.
